name: Pink Bourbon

migrationPath.notes: Genetic origin as Ethiopian Landrace — exact accession unknown
Primary cultivation region; how it arrived is undocumented

notableProducers: Diego Parra (La Hacienda, Pitalito, Colombia — multiple named harvests per season)
Servio Botina (El Cedro, Purutal, San Agustín, Colombia)
Octavio Rueda (El Mirador, Palestina, Colombia — 4 separate harvests per season)
Jhonatan Pito (Dos Pinos, San Agustín, Colombia)
description: Pink Bourbon has been genetically identified as an Ethiopian Landrace variety, but the database is not yet large enough to be more specific about its origins. It is called 'Pink Bourbon' because of its distinctive pink cherry color, but it is not closely related to classic Bourbon despite the name. It grows primarily in and around Huila, Colombia. The variety is relatively new to the specialty market and is being actively studied — its exact lineage and how it arrived in Colombia remain undocumented. A notable feature of Pink Bourbon production is harvest-date lot separation: skilled producers like Octavio Rueda run four distinct named harvests from the same trees each season, each producing a meaningfully different cup profile.
